goal: Widget (and its /widgets/ demo) had no card-selecting board — it auto-revealed random cards on a button press. Add an interactive tap-to-pick board.
done: Reworked widget.html draw flow: choose spread (Single / Past-Present-Future) -> a fanned row of 7 face-down cards (.board/.bcard) -> user taps to pick (Single: tap 1 -> reveals; Three: tap 3 with a '0/3' counter -> all reveal). Removed the old one-tap Draw button. Reveal rendering, card deep-links, key phrases, 'Draw again' (now re-deals a fresh board), promo/backlink logic, EN/TH strings, and the moonmallow art all preserved. The /widgets/ live-preview iframe and the home widget band both point at widget.html so they inherit the board automatically.
verified: JS parses (new Function on the inline script). Headless render of initial board at 400px: 7 fanned face-down cards + 'Tap a card to reveal it' hint (Single) / 'Pick 3 cards' (Three). Reveal path is a near-verbatim reuse of the prior working draw() render. Interactive click-through left to founder manual QA (browser harness blocked by sandbox).
